headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one word underlined and one gap. From the list of words lettered A- E, choose the word that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentences

S1.qs: 1.	Ibrahim was full of **admiration** for Amina but she treated him with ..................
2.	The economist argued that the apparently **inconsequential** rise in the price of petrol had a .................. effect on the cost of living
3.	The exotic bird looked **resplendent**: its bright plumage contrasted with the rather .................. undergrowth
4.	Femi is very **presumptuous** quite unlike his late father who was a rather .................. man
5.	The new manager found the bustle and **excitement** of city life difficult to adjust to after living for almost a decade in a relatively .................... village!
6.	The policeman's statement was **explicit** while that of the suspect was .................
7.	The post-war generation were born in an era of **scarcity** but grew up in a period of .....................
8.	The minister warned his officers to be **frugal** and avoid ..................... expenditure
9.	The deputy's loyalty to the boss **unalloyed** but the confidence of the boss in him is ....................
10.	The **eventual** implementation of the plan deviated much from the ................... conception

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to E, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences

S2.qs: 11.	The kidnappers demanded a ................ of N50.000 before they would release the rich businessman
12.	The governor scolded the contractors for the poor ................. of the project
13.	His decision to terminate the agreement was ................ as he did not consult the other party
14.	More often than not, the law ................. on individual freedom
15.	Because of the hard time, many workers now live in a state of .................... poverty
16.	The threat that he would be expelled from school if he failed his examination again ..................... him into seriousness
17.	It is really a .................. the more you look, the less you see
18.	The new car is strikingly attractive but I very much doubt its .......................
19.	Bunmi should not have taken offence at Emeka’s seemingly .................. comments
20.	As Siaka was a first offender, the magistrate merely ................ and discharged him

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider the most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 21.	The accountant falsified the records in order to feather his own nest. This means that the accountant tried to

opts.q1: B. put a feather in his cap
opts.q3: D. avenge a wrong done to him by his boss

S3.qs: 22.	The cashier wanted to pull the wool over my eyes by making me sign for the money I didn't receive. This means that the cashier wanted to

opts.q2: C. rub my eyes with cotton wool

S3.qs: 23.	The taxi-driver smelt a rat when the policeman waved him to stop and so he sped off. This means that the taxi driver

opts.q0: A. suddenly saw a big rat

S3.qs: 24.	The proscription of the students’ union was to nip the planned demonstration in the bud. This means that the proscription was meant to

opts.q0: A. keep the students on the alert

S3.qs: 25.	The man’s decision to withdraw his daughter from school because of her misbehavior really amounts to cutting off his own nose to spite his face. This means that

opts.q0: A. the decision will not affect the daughter
opts.q1: B. it will cause the man much pain to his nose and face
opts.q2: C. the decision does the man no good at all
opts.q3: D. the decision will make the daughter sober

S3.qs: 26.	When the managing director promised to increase the wages of the workers in the new year, everybody knew that he was talking with his tongue in his cheek. This means that the managing director

opts.q2: C. was not sincere at all
opts.q3: D. hid his tongue while speaking

S3.qs: 27.	The officer urged the soldiers to gird up their loins for the task ahead. This means that soldiers should

opts.q0: A. wake up early the next morning

S3.qs: 28.	The government introduced belt tightening measures in the budget for the new year, This means that the measures were
29.	Jimmy is good company, but he sometimes blows his own trumpet. This means that Jimmy

opts.q3: D. is an expert in playing the trumpet

S3.qs: 30.	The teacher was reprimanded for turning a blind eye to the acts of rudeness of his pupils. This means that the teacher

opts.q0: A. did not pay attention to the pupils
opts.q1: B. behaved like a blind man
opts.q2: C. was responsible for the rudeness of his pupils
opts.q3: D. ignored the behaviour of his pupils

S4.headd: From the words lettered A to E below each of the following sentences, choose the word that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word as it is used in the sentence.

S4.qs: 31.	The journalist refused to **divulge** the source of his information
32.	As soon as Shehu realized that he has made a mistake, he did everything to **rectify** it
33.	Ada was **skeptical** about the story Ifeoma told her
34.	It appears that this government is not ready to tolerate any **adverse** criticism
35.	The lawyer remarked that the fact that the accused did nothing when the innocent boy was being lynched made him **culpable**
36.	The mineral resources of the country are yet to be fully **tapped**
37.	The boss is quite **inflexible** once she has made up her mind
38.	Spain was once a very powerful nation but her influence has **waned** over the years
39.	The labour leaders described the newly-introduced economic measures as **obnoxious**
40.	The politician made very **scurrilous** remarks about his opponent

S5.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that best completes each of the following sentences

S5.qs: 41.	The new bakery will ................. one thousand loaves of bread daily
42.	The old man’s illness was so serious that he was not expected to .................
43.	Ahmadu would have arrived earlier but he was ............... in heavy traffic
44.	Bola is a good friend of mine, but I am sometimes ................... by her careless attitude
45.	Customers are advised to settle their electricity bills promptly, otherwise supply may be ....................
46.	The boy needs a broad piece of paper and .................. to practise his drawing
47.	Mr. Johnson instructed his driver to ensure that the tank of the car is filled with ................... for the long journey the next day
48.	The policeman has gathered .................... to complete the investigation
49.	Udoh was ................. hasty in his decision to resign
50.	Doctor: 'How is the ulcer patient?' Nurse: 'He’s recovering ................... faster than we expected'
51.	Chukwu’s appraisal was one of the ................. in the department
52.	There were .................... infant deaths in the town last year than in previous years
53.	The last book is the ................ interesting of the books we have read this term
54.	The uncompromising politician remained ..................... a political wilderness for almost a decade
55.	It was revealed that the drainage system of the town has been ................. of use since it was constructed
56.	The young man came to Lagos .................. the intention of staying with his friend
57.	The school boys were told to refrain ................. smoking
58.	He is a very brilliant student so it is not surprising that he got a ....................
59.	The children inherited many houses from their father but they have sold off ..................
60.	Mrs Coker had an accident because she was ......................
61.	I found out recently that one can enjoy ................ without drinking
62.	The two cats are so similar that I cannot tell whether that one is ....................
63.	................. all the contestants, the judges then announced the winner
64.	His clothes were not only tattered but also ................ with oil
65.	I .............. at the hotel for two days when I was suddenly asked to leave
66.	Dupe .................... to have spoken so rudely to her mother
67.	Perhaps what Ade told us ................ true after all
68.	To have gone through a civil war .................... a terrible experience
69.	Umar and Yusuf went hunting today ...................?
70.	Bose shouldn’t have interrupted the teacher ................?
71.	..................... are seven female teachers in our school
72.	The principal sent them home because they did not pay ................... school fees

S6.headd: In the following passages, the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, five choices are offered in columns lettered A to E. For each question, choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passage.
PA: Farming is a very common --73-- among the rural dwellers in the West African sub-region. Most often, this is practised at --74-- level with little if any --75-- outlay involved. Year in year out, the farmers eke out their --76-- from the land tilled with hoes and such other simple implements.
Many of these rural dwellers have never heard about --77-- farming let alone the prerequisites for the successful operation of such --78-- enterprises. --79-- cultivation is widely practised.

This is a process where farmers abandon their parcels of land for a period of four to five years to allow such land to recover its natural --80-- for better --81-- --82-- such as yam, maize, rice, cowpea which serve as --83-- food are planted on --84-- basis, while --85-- crops are hardly grown.
Invariably the --86-- are inherited and passed from one generation to another. Where --87-- are paid at all, they are usually in kind.

S6.headd: In the following passages, the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, five choices are offered in columns lettered A to E. For each question, choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passage.
PB: The --88-- has just completed his --89-- into a case of --90-- brought by a --91--, but was undecided as to whether or not there was enough evidence to --92-- the --93--. However, his inspector felt that there were sufficient ingredients in the case to warrant --94--.
Not only were there witnesses to --95-- the evidence of the complainant but the accused had virtually confessed to the offence.

The courtroom was filled to capacity during the ensuing --96--, as the accused had employed the services of a legal luminary for his defence. During the --97-- it became clear to all present that the --98-- relied upon by the prosecution was very weak.
The defence counsel successfully tore the evidence of the witnesses into shreds. The presiding --99-- had no choice but to --100-- and acquit the accused for want of evidence.
